text: In this video, we are presenting a case of robotic partial nephrectomy with the use of SIVRA-III-D virtual reality model in pre-operative planning of the procedure. SIVRA software creates the three-D models from existing preoperative CT scans or MRIs. It is considered a medical device and it is FDA cleared for both preoperative and intraoperative use. The models may be viewed in a standard three-D format from the surgeon's mobile phone or tablet and may be viewed in a virtual reality for even greater three-D visualization. Our patient is a fifty-two year old female who presented with an incidental finding of a large right renal mass. Imaging showed a double arterial supply and venous drainage of the kidney. A three-D model was created and provided us with a clearer, easy, memorable image of the vascular anatomy of the tumor and the kidney. It demonstrated a separate arterial supply and venous drainage of the tumor and gave us a better idea about the tumor relation to the normal renal burring Depending on our understanding of the anatomy provided by the three-D model, we planned to perform an early ligation and division of the superior artery and vein supplying the tumor, before claiming the inferior vascular group to minimize ischemia time.

The surgeon mobilizes the right colon to access the retroperitoneum, tracing the ureter proximally to the renal hilum. The superior vein draining the tumor is clipped and divided, followed by clipping and dividing the main stem and branches of the superior artery supplying the tumor.

The surgeon uses intravenous indocyanine green and fluorescence imaging to confirm no blood flow to the tumor while maintaining excellent blood flow to the normal kidney tissue. Intraoperative ultrasound is employed to delineate the tumor margins. Ischemia is induced by clamping the inferior artery and vein, followed by sharp dissection to excise the tumor.

The surgeon completes the inner layer of renography by oversewing the tumor bed. Early unclamping is performed, resulting in a total warm ischemia time of eleven minutes. The outer layer of renography is finished using interrupted sutures and a sliding clip technique.
The surgeon completes final hemostasis by applying flow seal and tail seal. The excised specimen is placed in an endocatch bag for removal, and a GP drain is inserted into the surgical site.
